description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a beige upper and lower wing structure, supported by visible struts and rigging wires. The fuselage is streamlined and painted in a muted gray, with a wooden propeller mounted at the nose. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all in matching beige. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and exposed. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely accommodating a single pilot. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, with a focus on lightweight construction and simple, functional lines.
description: A beige and gray vintage biplane with open cockpit, wooden propeller, and fixed landing gear, featuring dual wings and classic early aviation design.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the dual beige wings, tail structure, and the wooden propeller at the front.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the stacked biplane wings, open cockpit, and the propeller at the nose.
description_view_2: Opposite top-down view, similar to view 0, showing the wings, tail, and propeller from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, landing gear, and the rigging wires between the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts and rigging wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
